level_signals: 5+ years of product management experience required
Single-threaded product owner across multiple complex domains
Expected to drive cross-team alignment and influence without direct authority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of product management experience, ideally in platform, infrastructure, or security domains
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in platform, infrastructure, or security domains

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have experience working on account systems, identity, permissions, or data models in B2B SaaS
You are comfortable operating in technical problem spaces and partnering closely with engineering
You have experience driving cross-team alignment and influencing without direct authority
You can balance short-term execution with long-term platform strategy
You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and defining structure in complex environments
You communicate clearly and can articulate tradeoffs to both technical and non-technical audiences

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with identity and access management (IAM) systems
Familiarity with security, compliance, or abuse prevention
Experience supporting enterprise or regulated environments
Experience building platform APIs or shared services
Experience mentoring PMs or leading cross-functional initiatives

scope.surface: Platform Integrity & Protection (PAA) and Accounts — covering account security, abuse prevention, organization modeling, authentication, and enterprise compliance
primary_metrics_inferred: Account security and abuse prevention rates
Enterprise customer adoption of org/account features
core_responsibilities: Own and drive product strategy and roadmap across Platform Integrity & Protection and Accounts
Define and evolve Klaviyo's account and organization model to support enterprise scale and complexity
Lead initiatives focused on account security, authentication abuse prevention, and risk mitigation
Partner deeply with IAM to align authentication, authorization, and identity systems with account modeling
Collaborate with Security, Compliance, Legal, and GTM teams to meet regulatory and enterprise requirements
Align multiple engineering teams around shared data models, platform capabilities, and security posture
Define and track metrics for account security, abuse prevention, and platform health
Enable other product teams by building shared frameworks (e.g., permissions, lifecycle hooks, settings systems)
Drive clarity and alignment across stakeholders on tradeoffs between security, usability, and scalability

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear single-threaded product ownership across well-defined domains
Explicit acknowledgment that close but not exact matches are encouraged to apply
High-impact, cross-functional scope with visibility to Security, Compliance, and GTM
Equity, bonus, and comprehensive benefits mentioned as part of total compensation
red_flags: 5 days/week in-office in Boston is a strict requirement — eliminates all remote and hybrid candidates
Very broad scope (Platform Integrity AND Accounts) with single-threaded ownership may indicate under-resourcing
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship
10% travel requirement adds additional flexibility constraints

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years requirement, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated directly in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and B2B SaaS mention)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from statement about cost of labor across U.S. geographic markets)
ambiguities: The JD states 'ideally in platform, infrastructure, or security domains' as a qualifier for the 5+ years requirement — this softens the domain preference but the years floor is hard. Treated the domain qualifier as soft and years as hard.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; classified as 'unclear'.
